Notes:
Ticket Summary – SharePoint Folder Access (William McGinnis)

Per Colleen Rozen’s request, set up a test environment to validate granting restricted SharePoint access to a single, specific folder under Community Management. Configured permissions so the user would only have access to the designated folder and no visibility to other files or folders.

Following confirmation, Colleen requested to proceed with granting access. Access was then officially granted to William McGinnis for the specified folder only, and Colleen was notified once completed.

All requested actions completed. Ticket resolved.

Title: Spam
Notes: Reviewed Defender Explorer details for postmaster messages delivered to Collections. Confirmed messages are automated NDRs generated by Microsoft in response to rejected outbound emails. Collections mailbox is only the recipient of NDRs and did not originate the messages. Original sender must be identified from the NDR message headers and is likely an automated system or SMTP relay using Collections as the return address

Title: RE: Spam
Notes:
Ticket Summary: Undeliverable / Spam Email Notifications

User reported receiving multiple “Undeliverable: We are Processing the Payment…” emails and was concerned they were sent from their mailbox. Investigation confirmed the emails were external delivery failure (bounce) notifications, not messages sent by the user or Vintage Groupe.

Analysis of message details (including INKY metadata) shows the emails originated from an external domain (rstcpa.com), with SPF, DKIM, and DMARC passing for that sender. No outbound messages were found in Microsoft Exchange Message Trace, confirming the emails did not originate from the tenant.

INKY correctly flagged the messages due to payment-related content and external origin. User was advised that their account was not compromised, no action is required, and the messages can be safely ignored or deleted. Safe Sender allow-listing was not recommended to avoid weakening email security.

Title: FW: Stefanie Garcia - Increase Storage for Outlook

I coordinated with Manisha to purchase the E3 license, and once it became available, I assigned it to Stefanie to resolve the storage issue.

Title: Automatic reply: Auto Payment Set Up
Notes:
• Investigated reported fraudulent email activity from ACH@vintagegroupre.com.

  • Confirmed the address is a shared mailbox, not a distribution list.

  • Verified mailbox permissions:

    • Full Access: jaime@vintagegroupre.com

    • No Send As or Send on Behalf permissions assigned to any users.

  • Message trace confirmed emails were automatic replies generated by Microsoft Exchange Online, triggered when external senders emailed the ACH mailbox.

  • No user login activity, no credential compromise, and no security breach identified.

  • Identified two external recipients who received the automatic replies.

  • Presented findings to the client and recommended disabling automatic replies and restricting external senders.

  • Client requested complete deletion of the ACH mailbox.

  • Follow-up sent to client requesting confirmation on whether a mailbox backup is required prior to permanent deletion.

  • Awaiting client confirmation before proceeding with deletion.

Title: Vintage Group - Bounced back email address
Notes: After reviewing the Microsoft 365 Admin portal and Message Trace, the emails were marked as "Delivered" but not received by the recipient. The issue appears to be on the recipient's server, possibly due to spam filters, temporary server issues, or network delays. Despite retry attempts, the emails failed to be received. I recommend the recipient check their spam/junk folder.

Title: FW: Microsoft Office - Zero day
Notes: Received email from Colleen regarding "Immediate Action Needed: Office 365 Vulnerability (CVE-2026-21509)." I've reviewed the Microsoft advisory and shared the step-by-step action plan to address the issue, sending Colleen the step-by-step information, hence closing this ticket.

Title: FW: 29755 Courage Court: DRA - Plans to be placed in the mail.
Notes: Performed comprehensive troubleshooting on velta@vintagegroupre.com internal domain email delivery failures despite internal routing. Verified mailbox quota not exceeded, reviewed all Mail flow rules showing no matching blocks/DLP policies affecting vintagegroupre.com domain traffic, adjusted Default inbound Anti-spam policy Connection filter confirming sender IPs not listed in blocks, checked Quarantine (Bulk/Spam tabs) finding no held messages.  Executed fresh Message Trace showing persistent "Filtered"/"Failed" status on internal messages with no specific SMTP error codes identifying root cause. All standard Exchange Online mail flow components verified normal but delivery issue persists unresolved.

Title: FW: 29755 Courage Court: DRA - Plans to be placed in the mail.
Notes: I reviewed the emails and traced the sender and recipient mail flow for the given period. All messages were successfully delivered, with no failed or bounced emails. I shared the report with the user, including screenshots.
